Elephant Monitoring System

IoT-Based Farm Protection Project

📖 About the Project

The Elephant Monitoring System is an IoT-based real-time alert system designed to protect farmlands from elephant intrusions in rural Karnataka. Using ultrasonic sensors, a NodeMCU (ESP8266) microcontroller, and the ThingSpeak cloud platform, the system detects elephant proximity and instantly notifies farmers through Telegram alerts and this web dashboard — in both Kannada and English.

⚙️ How It Works

Ultrasonic sensors mounted at the farm perimeter continuously measure distance. When an object (elephant) enters within the threshold range, the NodeMCU sends a detection signal along with the distance value to ThingSpeak via Wi-Fi. The dashboard refreshes every 15 seconds and triggers visual alerts, sound, and vibration on the farmer's phone. Simultaneously, a Telegram bot sends instant messages so farmers are notified even when away from the dashboard.

🔧 Components Used

NodeMCU ESP8266 Wi-Fi module  ·  HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Sensors  ·  16×2 LCD Display  ·  Buzzer  ·  LED indicators  ·  ThingSpeak IoT platform  ·  Telegram Bot API

📋 Elephant Safety Guidelines

Follow these rules when elephants are nearby

⚠️ Precautions When Elephants Are Nearby
  • Stay alert for signs like tracks, dung, and sounds
  • Maintain a safe distance (at least 100 meters)
  • Avoid loud noises and sudden movements
  • Do not travel alone near forest areas, especially at night
  • Drive cautiously and never disturb elephants
🐘 What To Do If You Encounter an Elephant
  • Stay calm and do not panic
  • Back away slowly without turning your back
  • Keep objects like trees between you and the elephant
  • Move diagonally away and avoid direct paths
  • Find safe cover if needed
🚫 What NOT To Do
  • Do not throw stones or objects
  • Do not attempt to scare or chase elephants
  • Do not block their path
  • Do not approach calves or herds
🛡️ Safe Deterrent Methods (Community Use)
  • Use controlled noise like drums or bells in groups
  • Install lights around villages or farms
  • Use chili-based deterrents (fences or smoke)
  • Immediately inform forest authorities
🌿 Promote coexistence — Respect elephant habitats and natural movement paths
